docs.title: Scoutbook
docs.text:
A curated list of tools, programme ideas, campsites, and links for Scouts.

➕ Submit an entry

docs.title: About
docs.text: This list is community-maintained. Anyone can suggest an entry using the link above — all submissions are reviewed before being added.

docs.title: Campsites
docs.text:
➕ Submit an entry

Sites marked with ⚜️ are run by Scout Adventures, part of The Scout Association.

docs.title: Lochgoilhead ⚜️
docs.text: A lochside centre in Loch Lomond and Trossachs National Park at Shelter Park, Lochgoilhead, with indoor accommodation for 136, camping areas, and water/land activities like kayaking, climbing, and hillwalking.

docs.title: Chelwood Campsite
docs.text: A 30-acre site hidden in the Avon countryside with broad camping fields, woodland edges, and a winding stream perfect for bivouacking.

docs.title: Great Tower ⚜️
docs.text: A 250-acre woodland site on Lake Windermere near Newby Bridge, with lodges, tented villages, camping, and activities like kayaking, climbing, and bushcraft.

docs.title: Avon Tyrrell Outdoor Centre
docs.text: Set in the New Forest National Park, this centre offers open camping space, activity sessions, and outdoor learning facilities.

docs.title: Braggers Wood
docs.text: A traditional woodland site close to the New Forest and coast, featuring modern facilities and a variety of on-site activities.

docs.title: Butchers Coppice
docs.text: A 7½-acre site in north-west Bournemouth with easy access to Poole, Christchurch, and Wimborne.

docs.title: Broadstone Warren ⚜️
docs.text: A 400-acre woodland site in Ashdown Forest near Forest Row, featuring extensive camping areas, activity zones, and residential accommodation for large groups.

docs.title: Belchamps Scout Activity Centre
docs.text: A 28-acre activity centre with open fields, three lodges, and a wide mix of adventurous activities for all ages.

docs.title: Gilwell Park ⚜️
docs.text: The home of Scouting! A 109-acre woodland site in Epping Forest near Chingford, offering camping fields, modern lodges, and activities like archery, climbing, and high ropes.

docs.title: King Edward VIII (Riffhams)
docs.text: A 4-hectare wooded site beside National Trust land in Little Baddow, just a few miles from Hylands Park.

docs.title: Prances Scout Campsite & Activity Centre
docs.text: Located near Wickham Bishops, this site offers large and small camping fields, patrol areas, and residential accommodation.

docs.title: Skreens Park Activity Centre
docs.text: Close to Chelmsford, Skreens Park combines modern facilities with a wide choice of adventurous and team-building activities.

docs.title: Thriftwood
docs.text: Set in 76 acres near Brentwood, Thriftwood features open and wooded camping areas plus activities like archery, climbing, and shooting.

docs.title: Fordell Firs ⚜️
docs.text: A 48-acre adventure centre near Dunfermline with woodland camping, dorms for 67, and over 20 activities including archery, climbing, and aerial runways.

docs.title: Bounds Green Scout Park
docs.text: An 8-acre park within the North Circular, easily reached from Bounds Green station and 30 minutes from central London.

docs.title: Danemead Campsite
docs.text: A 35-acre ancient woodland site near Broxbourne, perfect for backwoods activities and lightweight camps.

docs.title: Yr Hafod ⚜️
docs.text: A mountain hostel in Eryri (Snowdonia) National Park near Bethesda in the Ogwen Valley, sleeping 32 with kitchen, drying room, and direct access to peaks like Tryfan and Y Garn for hillwalking and climbing.

docs.title: Cranbury Park
docs.text: Two acres of parkland within the Cranbury Park Estate, open from March to November.

docs.title: Cricket Campsite
docs.text: Set in River Hamble Country Park, the site includes woodlands, fields, and clearings across over 400 acres.

docs.title: Ferny Crofts Scout Activity Centre
docs.text: A 31-acre site in the New Forest National Park offering camping for hundreds and a large range of outdoor activities.

docs.title: Harry's Island
docs.text: A four-acre private site near Burley, ideal for quiet, back-to-basics camping in the New Forest.

docs.title: Lyons Copse
docs.text: A versatile site for up to 700 campers with indoor accommodation and varied activities.

docs.title: Pinsent Campsite
docs.text: A 6-acre wooded site with individual areas and firepits, perfect for traditional or back-to-basics camping.

docs.title: Hopehill Campsite
docs.text: A peaceful site in North West Kent surrounded by woodland and grassy glades, ideal for camping and outdoor challenges.

docs.title: Youlbury ⚜️
docs.text: A 42-acre woodland site on Boars Hill near Oxford, offering camping fields, lodges for 86, and activities like climbing, archery, go-karts, and aerial challenges.

docs.title: Meggernie ⚜️
docs.text: A remote 110-acre estate in Glen Lyon near Aberfeldy, offering self-catering lodges for 30, wild camping, and activities like survival skills, gorge walking, and environmental studies.

docs.title: Hesley Wood
docs.text: 132 acres of fields and woodland with three lodges and 26 camping pitches set for exploration and adventure.

docs.title: Birchmere
docs.text: A flexible site suited for everything from small hike camps to week-long events, with easy access to local attractions.

docs.title: Polyapes Campsite
docs.text: Covers 30 acres of grassland and woodland on a high ridge, offering classic camping and indoor accommodation.

docs.title: Silver Cross
docs.text: A 15-acre woodland site with easy transport links and close access to the Gower Peninsula and Brecon Beacons.

docs.title: Furzefield
docs.text: A welcoming site near Arundel, set in woodland and meadow, run by local volunteers.

docs.title: External Providers
docs.text: ➕ Submit an entry

docs.title: Expeditions Wales
docs.text: Expeditions Wales is a friendly organisation that prides itself on delivering a quality service, focusing on the safety and enjoyment of its clients throughout. Expeditions Wales is licensed by the Adventurous Activities Licensing Service to provide Hill Walking and Trekking activities and is an Approved Activity Provider for The Duke of Edinburgh's Award Expedition section.

docs.title: Scout Association Links
docs.text: ➕ Submit an entry

docs.title: Scouts.org.uk
docs.text: The homepage of The Scout Association - a gateway to the various systems and information available.

docs.title: My Membership
docs.text: The replacement for Compass, sometimes referred to as SWAN (System Without A Name). Manage your details, disclosures training, reviews and more. Team Leaders can also manage their team members.

docs.title: Shops & Suppliers
docs.text: ➕ Submit an entry

docs.title: One Stop Scouting
docs.text: One Stop Scouting is an online retailer specialising in clothing, equipment, and accessories for Scouts and Guides across the UK. Its product range covers everything from uniforms, scarves, and badges to outdoor kit, making it a convenient one-stop shop for leaders and young people alike.

docs.title: Tools and Apps
docs.text: ➕ Submit an entry

docs.title: Canva
docs.text: Canva is a web-based graphic design platform that allows users to create a wide range of visual content — including posters, presentations, social media graphics, and documents — using an intuitive drag-and-drop interface. It offers thousands of customisable templates and is popular with both individuals and organisations looking to produce professional-looking designs without specialist software or skills. For registered charities, they also offer their Pro package for free - see Canva for Nonprofits for information.

docs.title: Google for Nonprofits
docs.text: Google for Nonprofits is a programme that gives eligible charitable organisations free access to a suite of Google products, including Google Workspace, Google Ad Grants, the YouTube Nonprofit Programme, and Google Earth and Maps. It is designed to help nonprofits work more collaboratively, reach new supporters online, and communicate their impact more effectively.

docs.title: Microsoft365
docs.text: Microsoft for Nonprofits offers eligible charitable organisations up to 300 free Microsoft 365 Business Basic licences, giving teams access to web and mobile versions of Microsoft's core productivity apps, Teams, and 1 TB of OneDrive storage per user. Eligible organisations can also benefit from $2,000 of Microsoft Azure credits annually, providing access to Microsoft's cloud computing platform for hosting, data storage, and a wide range of other technical services.

docs.title: Online Scout Manager(OSM)
docs.text: Online Scout Manager (OSM) is a web-based administration platform designed by Scout leaders for Scout leaders, trusted by over 100,000 leaders across Scout, Guide, and Boys' Brigade associations worldwide. It brings together member records, badge tracking, programme planning, event management, financial tracking, and parent communications into a single secure system, helping leaders spend less time on paperwork and more time with young people.

docs.title: 34SP
docs.text: 34SP.com is a Manchester-based UK web hosting and domain registration provider that has been serving small businesses, developers, charities, and educational organisations for over 20 years. It offers a range of hosting plans — including WordPress, professional, and reseller options — and is particularly well regarded for its in-house customer support team, which can be reached by phone, email, or Slack seven days a week. 34SP generously provide free hosting for UK Charities - see their website for details.

docs.title: Brixly
docs.text: Brixly is an award-winning UK-based web hosting provider offering a range of services including shared web hosting, reseller hosting, VPS, and dedicated servers, all built on a high-performance NVMe cloud infrastructure powered by 100% renewable energy. It is particularly well regarded in the reseller hosting market and also offers a free hosting programme for charities, making it a popular choice for organisations of all sizes.
